C[Tom Newman: Good luck, and have fun.
C[Kuroneko: yeah, i think it's considering this a correspondence game
C[Kuroneko: i wanted to see how it handles tournament games with faster time controls
Kuroneko: because i know it gives you a warning when a game is starting, etc.
C[Kuroneko: if it's OK, what we'll do is start a second tournament with faster time controls
Kuroneko: because i want to see how the server handles those
arnfasta: kk so should i just resign now?
Kuroneko: so i know how to instruct kitties
Kuroneko: yeshu, if you don't mind
Kuroneko: it's working fine so far
Kuroneko: so we can try another game
C[Kuroneko: we can just play for another 10-15 moves, doesn't have to be a long game
Kuroneko: you can do correspondence style two-step moves in a real-time game too
Kuroneko: or two step submission rather
Kuroneko: where you select a point, then have to hit the submit button to confirm your move
Kuroneko: which is what correspondence servers do
C[Kuroneko: yeshu, i'm really really liking this
Kuroneko: i think we're definitely on to something
Kuroneko: and you can set conditional moves, even in a real-time game!
Kuroneko: i'm totally in on this idea now
Kuroneko: think we may finally have the means to carry it out
Kuroneko: so we'll consult more later
C[Kuroneko: just the integrated tournament system alone was a big deal
Kuroneko: but we're looking at a more complete package
C[Kuroneko: and i was just thinking ""now if only the board could take up more of the page..."", except i then realized that i'm still on a 14"" lappy XD
C[Kuroneko: going to test the pause function real quick
arnfasta: I can resume it as well
arnfasta: it's a very clean interface too
Kuroneko: one of the interesting things about its premise is that they borrowed a page from kaya in trying to hybridize real-time and correspondence games
Kuroneko: except they went one step further and integrated the two more seamlessly, so they're not as separate
C[Kuroneko: so things like conditional moves can actually be used in a real-time game
Kuroneko: which was never possible before
arnfasta: haven't really tried the conditional moves feature yet
Kuroneko: are you familiar with how it works?
arnfasta: trying it myself and not sure how to make it work
Kuroneko: it's disabled in this game
Kuroneko: which you'll understand why in a minute
Kuroneko: the idea is that, in a correspondence game
Kuroneko: there's a lot of ""obvious"" moves that are played
Kuroneko: and conditional moves allow you to set up a sequence in advance
Kuroneko: so you can set a string of, for example: black B-7, white A-9, black B-2, white D-15
arnfasta: that makes so much sense
Kuroneko: if you're playing white, and black plays B-7, the server will automatically play A-9 for you
Kuroneko: same with black B-2 and white D-15
Kuroneko: so you can save a ton of time in places like the endgame
C[Kuroneko: i believe it originated from correspondence chess servers
Kuroneko: and go servers like OGS started adopting it
Kuroneko: the problem is that, to set up conditional moves, you can also play out sequences on a board
Kuroneko: e.g. playing out a difficult sequence
Kuroneko: so if you disable game analysis (which is what that's meant for), disabling conditional moves is a natural extension of that
C[Kuroneko: because conditional moves can be used for the same purpose
C[Kuroneko: (you get a board that you can play out moves on)
Kuroneko: also, anoek says there's an update inbound
Kuroneko: so you may have to refresh in a minute
C[Kuroneko: (the devs give a heads up in the chat when they're pushing an update)
C[arnfasta: thought I was okay for a bit but I lost it when I tried to lock down what I'd setup

Kuroneko: you're talking about the center position?
arnfasta: the lower right center of the board
arnfasta: thought I had a decent position
arnfasta: but I didn't know how to lock it down
Kuroneko: just to make sure the boards aren't reversed
Kuroneko: the orientation is flipped because of the opposing colors
